student_review: Renowned for academic excellence and historic facilities.
financial_support_info: Offers bursaries for annual household incomes under £32,500,
sports: Extensive college and university-level sport, including competitive inter-college leagues and university clubs across many sports. Facilities and club information are typically coordinated through Oxford University Sport.
art_music: Strong arts and music scene across colleges and the university, including choirs, orchestras, ensembles, theatre and exhibitions. Many events are run through colleges, university departments and student groups.
societies: Large range of student societies and common rooms across colleges, plus university-wide clubs covering academic, cultural, volunteering and hobby interests.
job_opportunities_part_time: Part-time work opportunities are commonly available in Oxford via college roles, university departments, local hospitality/retail, and casual event work; students should follow university guidance on working hours during term.
job_opportunities_full_time: Full-time opportunities are supported through the university careers service, internships and graduate schemes, with strong recruitment pipelines into academia, consulting, finance, tech and the public sector.
nightlife_info: Oxford has a relaxed but lively nightlife with pubs, bars, student nights, and occasional club events.

student_review: Known for academic excellence and historic colleges.
financial_support_info: Provides bursaries and scholarships for low-income students and contextual offers.
sports: Wide range of sports through colleges and university clubs, including competitive varsity and inter-college competitions. Sports facilities and club coordination are supported through the university sports service.
art_music: Active music and arts community with choirs, orchestras, bands, theatre and visual arts across colleges and university venues.
societies: Hundreds of student-led societies spanning academic interests, cultural groups, volunteering, entrepreneurship and hobbies, many coordinated through the students’ union and colleges.
job_opportunities_part_time: Part-time work is often available via colleges, university departments and local employers (hospitality/retail/tutoring); students should consider course workload and term-time working guidance.
job_opportunities_full_time: Graduate and full-time opportunities are supported via the university careers service, with strong pipelines into research, tech, engineering, consulting, finance and public service.
nightlife_info: Cambridge nightlife is mostly centred on pubs and student events, with a smaller club scene than major cities.

student_review: Strong STEM programs with a challenging workload.
financial_support_info: Offers scholarships and hardship funds for eligible students.
sports: Extensive sports clubs and competitive teams, with opportunities ranging from recreational to performance sport. Sports are largely organized through Imperial College Union clubs.
art_music: Active performing arts and music groups (e.g., bands, orchestras, choirs, theatre, dance) alongside London’s wider cultural scene.
societies: Large number of student societies including academic/departmental, cultural, arts, volunteering and tech/entrepreneurship groups, mainly through Imperial College Union.
job_opportunities_part_time: Part-time work opportunities are widely available in London (hospitality, retail, tutoring, campus roles). Students often balance work with demanding course schedules.
job_opportunities_full_time: Strong full-time prospects supported by Imperial careers services and industry links, especially in engineering, tech, consulting and finance.
nightlife_info: London offers one of the best nightlife scenes in the UK, with countless clubs, bars, gigs and late-night events.

student_review: Diverse courses with vibrant campus life.
financial_support_info: Financial support includes scholarships and bursaries for eligible students.
sports: Wide selection of sports clubs, fitness and recreational activities, with student participation supported through UCL Students’ Union. London location also provides access to external clubs and facilities.
art_music: Many music, theatre and arts opportunities via student groups and London’s cultural institutions (museums, galleries, theatres).
societies: Hundreds of societies spanning academic subjects, cultural communities, campaigning, volunteering, entrepreneurship and hobbies, coordinated via UCL Students’ Union.
job_opportunities_part_time: Part-time opportunities are plentiful across London and through campus jobs (where available), including tutoring, hospitality and retail; students should follow guidance on term-time hours.
job_opportunities_full_time: Full-time and graduate opportunities are supported through UCL Careers, with strong access to London-based employers across finance, consulting, tech, healthcare, government and NGOs.
nightlife_info: Central London gives UCL students easy access to a huge range of nightlife, from pubs to major clubs and live music.

student_review: Historic campus with strong research output.
financial_support_info: Offers scholarships, bursaries, and student loans for support.
sports: Strong sports offering with clubs and recreational sport, supported through the university and students’ association. Facilities typically include gyms and sports centers with a range of activities.
art_music: Large arts and music community including ensembles, choirs, theatre, and festivals; Edinburgh’s city-wide arts scene is a major advantage.
societies: Wide range of societies and student groups through the students’ association, including academic, cultural, volunteering and interest-based clubs.
job_opportunities_part_time: Part-time roles are commonly available in Edinburgh (hospitality, retail, tourism, events) and sometimes via university departments; students should consider workload in term.
job_opportunities_full_time: Full-time opportunities supported by the university careers service, with graduate routes into tech, finance, public sector, education, and research.
nightlife_info: Edinburgh has a fun nightlife with pubs, cocktail bars and student nights, especially around the city centre.

student_review: Excellent programs with a central location.
financial_support_info: Provides scholarships, bursaries, and hardship support for students in need.
sports: Sports clubs and activities are available through King’s College London Students’ Union, with access to London-wide facilities and competitions.
art_music: Good access to music and arts through student groups and central London’s major cultural venues; opportunities include ensembles, performance and creative societies.
societies: Broad range of societies including course-related, cultural, faith, volunteering, campaigning, and hobby groups via KCLSU.
job_opportunities_part_time: Part-time work is widely available in central London (retail, hospitality, tutoring, events) and sometimes through campus opportunities; students should follow any visa/working-hour constraints if applicable.
job_opportunities_full_time: Full-time/graduate opportunities supported via King’s Careers and London employer networks, including healthcare, policy, law, finance, consulting and tech.
nightlife_info: Being in central London means KCL students can easily reach many of the city’s best nightlife spots.

student_review: Known for social sciences and strong career prospects.
financial_support_info: Offers scholarships, bursaries, and financial aid to students.
sports: Sports and fitness opportunities are available through LSE student clubs and London facilities; participation ranges from recreational to competitive.
art_music: Arts and music opportunities via student groups, plus exceptional access to London’s theatres, galleries and music venues.
societies: Extensive societies ecosystem (academic, cultural, political, media, volunteering, entrepreneurship) strongly associated with LSE’s social-science focus.
job_opportunities_part_time: Part-time work opportunities are widely available in London (events, hospitality, retail, tutoring). Students often find roles aligned with language skills, admin, research support or event staffing.
job_opportunities_full_time: Very strong full-time prospects supported by LSE Careers; common destinations include finance, consulting, policy/government, NGOs, research and tech.
nightlife_info: LSE’s location offers quick access to central London nightlife, including bars, clubs, and live events.

student_review: Diverse student body with vibrant city life.
financial_support_info: Provides scholarships and support programs for eligible students.
sports: Large range of sports clubs and facilities, with opportunities for beginners through to competitive teams, Student sport is often coordinated through the students’ union and university sport services.
art_music: Strong music and arts scene with student groups and venues across Manchester; opportunities include bands, orchestras, theatre and creative societies.
societies: Many societies spanning academic, cultural, faith, volunteering, media, entrepreneurship and hobbies, largely coordinated through the Students’ Union.
job_opportunities_part_time: Part-time jobs are commonly available in Manchester (retail, hospitality, events, tutoring) and potentially on-campus roles; the city’s size supports varied options.
job_opportunities_full_time: Full-time and graduate support via the university careers service, with regional and national employers across tech, engineering, healthcare, finance and the public sector.
nightlife_info: Manchester is famous for nightlife, with lots of student nights, clubs, bars and live music venues.

student_review: Strong academic programs with lively student community.
financial_support_info: Offers scholarships, bursaries, and hardship funds to students.
sports: Good selection of sports clubs and activities, from recreational sport to competitive teams, supported through the students’ union and university sport. Access to local outdoor activities around Bristol is also a benefit.
art_music: Active arts and music culture with student theatre, bands, ensembles and creative societies; Bristol’s city music scene is well known.
societies: Wide variety of societies including academic, cultural, volunteering, performing arts and hobby groups, largely coordinated through the students’ union.
job_opportunities_part_time: Part-time roles are commonly available across Bristol (hospitality, retail, events, tutoring) and sometimes through university departments; students should balance with study demands.
job_opportunities_full_time: Full-time prospects supported by the careers service with links to employers in engineering, aerospace, tech, creative industries, finance and the public sector.
nightlife_info: Bristol has a lively student nightlife with popular pubs, clubs, and regular live music events.

student_review: Welcoming atmosphere with strong research opportunities.
financial_support_info: Provides scholarships, bursaries, and funding options for students.
sports: Range of sports clubs and facilities with opportunities for social and competitive sport, coordinated through the university and student groups.
art_music: Active arts and music life including theatre, bands, ensembles and creative societies; Glasgow’s wider music and arts scene offers many options.
societies: Many student societies across academic interests, culture, volunteering, faith and hobbies, with strong student community structures.
job_opportunities_part_time: Part-time work is available across Glasgow (retail, hospitality, events) and potentially through university roles; students should account for timetable and workload.
job_opportunities_full_time: Full-time and graduate opportunities supported through the university careers service, with routes into engineering, tech, finance, education, healthcare and research.
nightlife_info: Glasgow has a vibrant nightlife with plenty of clubs, pubs, gig venues and student-friendly events.
